label: Early access / game preview
note: EA coverage requires different standards than a full review. Judge the current state, not the promised state.
opening_rule: First sentence: whether the current early access build is worth buying now -- not what the finished game might become.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: honest assessment of whether the developer can deliver on the roadmap -- based on track record and current build quality, not promises.

sections.name: Current state
sections.purpose: What exists in the build right now -- content, features, stability. Not what is planned. Be precise about version number and build date if available.

sections.name: Core loop
sections.purpose: Is the fundamental gameplay loop already engaging in its current form? If the game requires 'getting good' before it clicks -- say how long that takes and whether it is worth it.

sections.name: Technical state
sections.purpose: Performance, bugs, crashes, missing features. Be specific -- frame rates, hardware tested, frequency of issues. EA does not excuse broken fundamentals.

sections.name: Roadmap reality check
sections.purpose: What is promised vs what is delivered. Does the developer have a track record of delivering on EA promises? Name examples. Do not treat a roadmap as a guarantee.

sections.name: Buy now or wait
sections.purpose: Concrete recommendation based on current state only. Who should buy now and why -- not who might enjoy it when finished. If waiting is the right call, say so directly.
